changed.from: For every nu in M_tan and every epsilon > 0, there exists u_epsilon in U_nu and a bounded-support path h -> nu^(u_epsilon,h) from lem:path-existence with H_nu dot_pi_nu(u_epsilon)^2 / J_nu(u_epsilon) >= kappa_nu - epsilon such that every Alg in A_T satisfies sup_{h in {-h_T(nu,u_epsilon), +h_T(nu,u_epsilon)}} mathfrak_R_T(Alg, nu^(u_epsilon,h)) >= (universal positive constant) * (kappa_nu - epsilon) * log T for all sufficiently large T.
changed.to: For every nu in M_tan and every epsilon > 0, there exists u_epsilon in U_nu and a bounded-support path h -> nu^(u_epsilon,h) from lem:path-existence with H_nu dot_pi_nu(u_epsilon)^2 / J_nu(u_epsilon) >= kappa_nu - epsilon, and there exists eta>0, such that every Alg in A_T satisfies sup_{|h|<=eta} mathfrak_R_T(Alg, nu^(u_epsilon,h)) >= (universal positive constant) * (kappa_nu - epsilon) * log T for all sufficiently large T.
changed.reason: The two final-scale alternatives h=+-sqrt(log T/(T J_nu(u))) are too sparse to force logarithmic cumulative regret for every predictable design. A design that knows this pair can explore for order T log log T/log T rounds, distinguish the sign with error about 1/(log T)^2, and then track the corresponding oracle, paying only O(log log T) regret on that pair. The logarithmic lower bound is the cumulative local-estimation cost over a neighborhood, equivalently an epoched grid of local two-point problems, not a single final-scale pair.

changed.from: For every 0 < underline_m <= overline_m <= 1 and every underline_r > 0, inf_{Alg in A_T} sup_{nu in M(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r)} mathfrak_R_T(Alg,nu) >= (positive constant depending only on underline_m, overline_m, and underline_r) * log T for all sufficiently large T.
changed.to: For every 0 < underline_m < overline_m <= 1 and every underline_r > 0 such that the strict interior subclass M^circ={nu in M_tan: underline_m < m_a(nu) < overline_m and r_{a,nu} > underline_r for a=0,1} is nonempty, inf_{Alg in A_T} sup_{nu in M(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r)} mathfrak_R_T(Alg,nu) >= c(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r) log T for all sufficiently large T, with c=(universal constant) times sup_{nu in M^circ} kappa_nu up to a fixed numerical factor.
changed.reason: The original quantification includes empty or lower-dimensional bands. If underline_m=overline_m, then every law in the band has m_0=m_1=underline_m and the fixed half-half design has zero regret throughout the band, contradicting any positive logarithmic lower bound. If underline_r is infeasible, the class is empty. The local lower-bound path also needs strict slack to remain inside the closed regular band.

changed.from: Optimize K(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r) by replacing each J_{a,nu}(u_a) with its convex dual over bounded-support moment scores, then reduce the outer supremum over nu in M(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r) to finite-support extremal laws via moment geometry.
changed.to: Let rho(v)=sup { r(P): P is a law on [0,1], int y^2 dP(y)=v^2 }, where r(P)=inf_{b_0,b_1} int (y^2-b_0-b_1 y)^2 dP(y). Then the local duality gives kappa_nu=(m_0+m_1)/4 { r_{0,nu}m_1/m_0^4+r_{1,nu}m_0/m_1^4 }, and hence K(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)=sup over m_0,m_1 in [underline_m,overline_m] with rho(m_a)>=underline_r of (m_0+m_1)/4 { rho(m_0)m_1/m_0^4+rho(m_1)m_0/m_1^4 }. The envelope rho(v) is attained by a law supported on at most five points because r(P) depends only on moments through order four.
changed.reason: The arm score program has an exact Hilbert-space solution J_a(x)=x^2/r_a, so the class frontier reduces to a scalar bounded moment envelope for r_a at fixed second moment. This is the correct finite-dimensional frontier program exposed by the proof; the remaining unsolved part is evaluating rho(v) in closed algebraic form.

changed.from: Does K(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r) admit a closed-form expression attained by a finite-support extremal law, and hence determine the constant-exact minimax Neyman-regret frontier over M(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r)?
changed.to: K(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r) admits an algebraic closed-form moment-envelope expression attained by finite-support extremal arm laws, and hence determines the exact class-level local lower-bound complexity over M(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r). This lower-bound complexity alone does not determine the sharp universal multiplicative constant of the full minimax regret without a matching constant-sharp upper bound.
changed.reason: The frozen core proves a minimax lower-bound mechanism with an unspecified universal constant. It contains no matching constant-sharp upper-bound theorem, so the phrase constant-exact minimax Neyman-regret frontier is stronger than what can be derived. The closed-form K statement is the faithful solved content.

changed.from: Let rho(v)=sup { r(P): P is a law on [0,1], int y^2 dP(y)=v^2 }, where r(P)=inf_{b_0,b_1} int (y^2-b_0-b_1 y)^2 dP(y). Then the local duality gives kappa_nu=(m_0+m_1)/4 { r_{0,nu}m_1/m_0^4+r_{1,nu}m_0/m_1^4 }, and hence K(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)=sup over m_0,m_1 in [underline_m,overline_m] with rho(m_a)>=underline_r of (m_0+m_1)/4 { rho(m_0)m_1/m_0^4+rho(m_1)m_0/m_1^4 }. The envelope rho(v) is attained by a law supported on at most five points because r(P) depends only on moments through order four.
changed.to: For v in (0,1), set q=v^2 and let mu_v be the unique solution in (q,v) of mu^4-2mu^3+2qmu^2-2q^2mu+q^2=0. Define rho(v)=((mu_v-q)(q-mu_v^2))/(4mu_v(1-mu_v)) and rho(1)=0. The value rho(v) is attained by the three-point law on {0,x_v,1}, x_v=(mu_v^2-2mu_v q+q)/(2mu_v(1-mu_v)), with weights p_x=(mu_v-q)/(x_v(1-x_v)), p_1=(q-x_v mu_v)/(1-x_v), and p_0=(x_v+q-(1+x_v)mu_v)/x_v. Then K(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)=sup over m_0,m_1 in [underline_m,overline_m] with rho(m_a)>=underline_r of (m_0+m_1)/4 { rho(m_0)m_1/m_0^4+rho(m_1)m_0/m_1^4 }, and the supremum is attained by taking the two arm marginals to be the corresponding three-point extremal laws whenever the feasible scalar set is nonempty.
changed.reason: The prior handle reduced the frontier to the scalar envelope rho but did not evaluate rho. The proof of lem:rho-envelope-closed-form solves the Hausdorff moment problem explicitly and improves the finite-support bound from five points to three points per arm.

changed.from: For v in (0,1), set q=v^2 and let mu_v be the unique solution in (q,v) of mu^4-2mu^3+2qmu^2-2q^2mu+q^2=0. Define rho(v)=((mu_v-q)(q-mu_v^2))/(4mu_v(1-mu_v)) and rho(1)=0. The value rho(v) is attained by the three-point law on {0,x_v,1}, x_v=(mu_v^2-2mu_v q+q)/(2mu_v(1-mu_v)), with weights p_x=(mu_v-q)/(x_v(1-x_v)), p_1=(q-x_v mu_v)/(1-x_v), and p_0=(x_v+q-(1+x_v)mu_v)/x_v. Then K(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)=sup over m_0,m_1 in [underline_m,overline_m] with rho(m_a)>=underline_r of (m_0+m_1)/4 { rho(m_0)m_1/m_0^4+rho(m_1)m_0/m_1^4 }, and the supremum is attained by taking the two arm marginals to be the corresponding three-point extremal laws whenever the feasible scalar set is nonempty.
changed.to: For v in (0,1), set q=v^2 and let mu_v be the unique solution in (q,v) of mu^4-2mu^3+2qmu^2-2q^2mu+q^2=0. Define rho(v)=((mu_v-q)(q-mu_v^2))/(4mu_v(1-mu_v)) and rho(1)=0. Let F(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)={v in [underline_m,overline_m]: rho(v)>=underline_r}. For m_0,m_1 in F define Phi(m_0,m_1)=(m_0+m_1)/4 { rho(m_0)m_1/m_0^4+rho(m_1)m_0/m_1^4 }. Then K(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)=sup_{m_0,m_1 in F(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)} Phi(m_0,m_1). If F is nonempty, the supremum is a maximum and is attained by taking the two arm marginals to be the corresponding three-point laws on {0,x_v,1}, x_v=(mu_v^2-2mu_v q+q)/(2mu_v(1-mu_v)), with weights p_x=(mu_v-q)/(x_v(1-x_v)), p_1=(q-x_v mu_v)/(1-x_v), and p_0=(x_v+q-(1+x_v)mu_v)/x_v. If F is empty, the regular band is empty and no attainment law is asserted.
changed.reason: The proof derives the same algebraic frontier as the current handle, but the scalar feasible set F and the empty-class convention are load-bearing for a well-typed attainment statement.

directive: Fix the note by re-deriving the flagship converse without weakening it. Correct lem:neyman-gap-identity so its statement is exactly V(pi)-V(p)=S^2(pi-p)^2/[pi(1-pi)], with S=m0+m1 and p=pi*; the proof already uses this denominator. Then replace, not patch, the proof of lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk: abandon the single final-scale van Trees neighborhood, since it leaves the uncontrolled adaptive feedback term. Prove the local Omega(log T) bound by an epoched multi-scale construction. Partition times into geometric epochs of lengths n_k for k=1,...,Theta(log T). For each epoch construct a fresh local two-point Le Cam problem between the base law and a mean-preserving second-moment perturbation, preferably the linear-tilt path dnu_a^h=(1+h s_a)dnu_a, with scale h_k asymp n_k^{-1/2} so the oracle allocation shifts by Theta(h_k), the within-epoch KL/information is O(1) for every predictable design because outcomes are bounded and only epoch-length information is used, and testing remains bounded away from perfect. Convert the resulting unavoidable Theta(h_k) allocation uncertainty, via the corrected Neyman-gap identity, into Omega(n_k h_k^2)=Omega(1) expected cumulative Neyman loss in that epoch. Sum these self-contained epoch contributions to obtain Omega(log T), explicitly avoiding any cross-epoch information-amplification or burn-in claim. Make the argument valid uniformly for every predictable adaptive design, using Yao/Bayes or sup-risk averaging as needed so the summed epoch lower bounds imply the stated minimax lower bound over the tangent-regular band. Repair the proof wiring: add lem:neyman-gap-identity to lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk, add that lemma to thm:instance-local-minimax, and add lem:band-continuity-for-linear-tilts to thm:global-log-rate. Strengthen thm:instance-local-minimax's conclusion to expose the path witness as the linear-tilt path, so thm:global-log-rate can invoke the continuity lemma directly. Finally, revise prose: call the result the first matching-rate minimax Omega(log T) lower bound on the tangent-regular subclass, not a constant-sharp same-class theorem; state that the closed-form frontier K is solved, while the remaining OEQ is only the constant-sharp minimax upper bound.

Two targeted corrections (do NOT change any other node; the formula, epoched cumulative-risk argument, and wiring are already correct). (1) oeq:closed-form-frontier — the finite-support ATTAINMENT claim must be CONDITIONAL on the feasible moment set F(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)={v in [underline_m,overline_m]: rho(v)>=underline_r} being NONEMPTY. Restate: the closed-form moment-envelope expression K=sup_{m0,m1 in F} Phi(m0,m1) holds unconditionally; the supremum is ATTAINED by the explicit three-point extremal arm laws ONLY WHEN F is nonempty; when F is empty the regular band M(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r) is empty and NO attainment law is asserted. Do not claim unconditional attainment. (2) thm:global-log-rate positioning/prose — describe the result honestly as the FIRST matching-RATE (Omega(log T)) one-sided minimax LOWER bound on the tangent-regular subclass, matching the published O(log T) upper RATE; it is NOT a two-sided constant-sharp minimax theorem. State explicitly that the closed-form class frontier K is SOLVED, and the only remaining open object (the OEQ) is the constant-sharp minimax UPPER bound matching K up to O(1).
note: D0.5 round-2 findings routed back: oeq empty-band attainment guard + global-log-rate one-sided positioning

changed.from: K(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r) admits an algebraic closed-form moment-envelope expression attained by finite-support extremal arm laws, and hence determines the exact class-level local lower-bound complexity over M(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r). This lower-bound complexity alone does not determine the sharp universal multiplicative constant of the full minimax regret without a matching constant-sharp upper bound.
changed.to: K(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r) admits the algebraic closed-form moment-envelope expression K=sup_{m_0,m_1 in F(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)} Phi(m_0,m_1), where F={v in [underline_m,overline_m]: rho(v)>=underline_r}. If F is nonempty, the supremum is a maximum and is attained by the explicit finite-support extremal arm laws from def:frontier-duality-handle; if F is empty, M(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r) is empty and no attainment law is asserted. This determines the exact class-level local lower-bound complexity over M(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r). This lower-bound complexity alone does not determine the sharp universal multiplicative constant of the full minimax regret without a matching constant-sharp upper bound.
changed.reason: The current wording makes finite-support attainment sound unconditional. When F is empty, the regular band is empty, so there is no law at which an attainment statement could be true. The closed-form K identity remains unconditional, while attainment must be conditional on F being nonempty.

directive: Correct the curvature normalization throughout (preserve the flagship matching-RATE claim; this rescales the complexity constant/frontier, NOT the log T rate, and adds NO new headline hypothesis). In lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk, remove the false universal lower bound g(pi) >= c_H H_nu (pi-p)^2 on M_tan. Use the exact identity g(pi)=S^2 (pi-p)^2/[pi(1-pi)] and the uniform inequality 1/[pi(1-pi)] >= 4 to obtain the honest universal bound g(pi) >= 4 S^2 (pi-p)^2 (S=m0+m1). Define and propagate the corrected local complexity kappa_nu^corr := S^2 * sup_u dot_pi(u)^2/J_nu(u) = p(1-p) kappa_nu. Update thm:instance-local-minimax to state the instance lower bound as R_T >= c_0 (kappa_nu^corr - eps) log T with universal constants, and ADD lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk to its depends_on. Propagate the same S^2-scaled complexity into thm:global-log-rate and the closed-form frontier K (and lem:kappa-rayleigh-formula / def:local-complexity / def:frontier-duality-handle / lem:local-complexity-closed-form as needed). On the interior band note explicitly that m_a >= underline_m gives S^2 >= 4 underline_m^2 > 0, so the global minimax lower-bound rate remains Omega(log T); do NOT weaken the flagship matching-rate claim. Revise novelty positioning to acknowledge Chen-Ge-Qian-Harshaw, arXiv:2511.19905, "Sigmoid-FTRL: Design-Based Adaptive Neyman Allocation for AIPW Estimators" (Nov 2025), as a DESIGN-BASED (fixed potential outcomes) AIPW adaptive-design UPPER-bound/algorithm paper; narrow our claim to SUPERPOPULATION HORVITZ-THOMPSON cumulative variance regret lower bounds plus the closed-form frontier; contrast HT vs AIPW and superpopulation vs design-based randomness; keep Neopane/Noarov as the HT upper-bound rate comparators.
note: D0.5 round-3: dropped_factor curvature fix (H_nu->S^2, rate survives), missing_dep, novelty narrowing vs 2511.19905

changed.from: kappa_nu = sup_{u in U_nu} H_nu dot_pi_nu(u)^2 / J_nu(u).
changed.to: Let S_nu=m_0+m_1. Define kappa_nu = sup_{u in U_nu} S_nu^2 dot_pi_nu(u)^2 / J_nu(u). Equivalently, this corrected complexity equals pi_nu_star(1-pi_nu_star) times the curvature-scaled quotient previously written with H_nu.
changed.reason: The exact identity is g_nu(pi)=S_nu^2(pi-pi_nu_star)^2/[pi(1-pi)]. Uniformly over all adaptive allocations pi in (0,1), this yields g_nu(pi)>=4S_nu^2(pi-pi_nu_star)^2. The H_nu curvature is the local second derivative at the oracle and overstates the globally valid lower-bound normalization by the factor 1/[pi_nu_star(1-pi_nu_star)]. The minimax log rate is unchanged, but the constant/frontier must use the S_nu^2-scaled information quotient.

changed.from: For v in (0,1), set q=v^2 and let mu_v be the unique solution in (q,v) of mu^4-2mu^3+2qmu^2-2q^2mu+q^2=0. Define rho(v)=((mu_v-q)(q-mu_v^2))/(4mu_v(1-mu_v)) and rho(1)=0. Let F(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)={v in [underline_m,overline_m]: rho(v)>=underline_r}. For m_0,m_1 in F define Phi(m_0,m_1)=(m_0+m_1)/4 { rho(m_0)m_1/m_0^4+rho(m_1)m_0/m_1^4 }. Then K(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)=sup_{m_0,m_1 in F(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)} Phi(m_0,m_1). If F is nonempty, the supremum is a maximum and is attained by taking the two arm marginals to be the corresponding three-point laws on {0,x_v,1}, x_v=(mu_v^2-2mu_v q+q)/(2mu_v(1-mu_v)), with weights p_x=(mu_v-q)/(x_v(1-x_v)), p_1=(q-x_v mu_v)/(1-x_v), and p_0=(x_v+q-(1+x_v)mu_v)/x_v. If F is empty, the regular band is empty and no attainment law is asserted.
changed.to: For v in (0,1), set q=v^2 and let mu_v be the unique solution in (q,v) of mu^4-2mu^3+2qmu^2-2q^2mu+q^2=0. Define rho(v)=((mu_v-q)(q-mu_v^2))/(4mu_v(1-mu_v)) and rho(1)=0. Let F(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)={v in [underline_m,overline_m]: rho(v)>=underline_r}. For m_0,m_1 in F define Phi(m_0,m_1)={rho(m_0)m_1^2/m_0^3+rho(m_1)m_0^2/m_1^3}/[4(m_0+m_1)]. Then K(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)=sup_{m_0,m_1 in F(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r)} Phi(m_0,m_1). If F is nonempty, the supremum is a maximum and is attained by taking the two arm marginals to be the corresponding three-point laws on {0,x_v,1}, x_v=(mu_v^2-2mu_v q+q)/(2mu_v(1-mu_v)), with weights p_x=(mu_v-q)/(x_v(1-x_v)), p_1=(q-x_v mu_v)/(1-x_v), and p_0=(x_v+q-(1+x_v)mu_v)/x_v. If F is empty, the regular band is empty and no attainment law is asserted.
changed.reason: After correcting def:local-complexity from H_nu to S_nu^2, the Rayleigh formula changes from (m_0+m_1)/4 {r_0 m_1/m_0^4+r_1 m_0/m_1^4} to {r_0 m_1^2/m_0^3+r_1 m_0^2/m_1^3}/[4(m_0+m_1)]. The scalar moment envelope rho(v) is unchanged, but the class frontier Phi must be rescaled accordingly.

directive: Reframe ONLY the novelty positioning/prose of thm:global-log-rate and thm:instance-local-minimax to clear a below-flagship-floor decision-referee finding (the MATH is already correct and passed; do NOT change any statement, proof, constant, or the log T rate). Frame the contribution as flagship via axis (c) = the FIRST nontrivial minimax LOWER bound for a named estimand (cumulative Neyman regret of the adaptive Horvitz-Thompson estimator) that previously lacked one, PLUS a rate-match to the published O(log T) upper bounds (Neopane 2024 ClipSMT; Noarov 2025) — NOT as a constant-sharp two-sided minimax theorem. State explicitly: the universal minimax RATE is log T; the named interior moment/overlap band + strong-identification (tangent-strength/Fisher-information) conditions are STANDARD named regularity whose fixed radii control only the frontier CONSTANT K (which is SOLVED in closed form), and the ONLY open object is the constant-sharp minimax value (matching constant-sharp UPPER bound), left as an OEQ. In the instance theorem's Gap prose, cite Chen-Ge-Qian-Harshaw (arXiv:2511.19905) as the neighboring DESIGN-BASED AIPW result and narrow our firstness to the SUPERPOPULATION HORVITZ-THOMPSON objective (contrast HT vs AIPW, superpopulation vs design-based). Keep Neopane/Noarov as the HT upper-bound rate comparators. Do NOT weaken the theorem; only reframe positioning.
note: D0.5: flagship reframe (axis-c first-bound + rate-match; named regularity; K solved, constant-sharp=OEQ; narrow vs 2511.19905)

directive: Discharge five proof-rigor gaps (the MATH claim and the field-tier framing are correct; these are rigor/wiring, do NOT weaken any statement). (a) self_bounding_threshold_gap in lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk: make the self-bounding threshold EXPLICIT — set t_* = C log T (C a universal constant), and note the harmonic sum sum_{t=t_*}^{T} 1/t = log T - log(C log T) = (1-o(1)) log T, so the Omega(log T) bound is discharged; the fallback B_T=O(log T) makes L sqrt(t B_{t-1}) <= L sqrt(t * A d^2 J^{-1} log T) = o(t) for t >= C log T, giving denominator <= 2Jt on [t_*,T]. State t_* = Theta(log T) explicitly, not an instance-fixed threshold. (b) dependency_overreach in thm:global-log-rate: thm:instance-local-minimax now exposes the linear-tilt path witness in its conclusion; invoke lem:band-continuity-for-linear-tilts on THAT linear-tilt path and add it to depends_on. (c) neighborhood_restriction_gap in thm:global-log-rate: after shrinking eta to eta<=min(eta_0,eta_1) to stay inside the closed band, note the local lower bound of lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk holds for EVERY fixed eta>0 (with T_0 depending on eta), so the Omega(log T) bound survives on the smaller neighborhood — state this explicitly. (d) attainment_continuity_gap in oeq:closed-form-frontier: the sup->max upgrade uses continuity of rho and Phi and compactness of the feasible set F; add lem:rho-envelope-closed-form (which proves rho continuous on (0,1]) to depends_on and state the compactness/continuity argument for the max. (e) global_rate_overclaim in oeq:closed-form-frontier: state precisely that thm:global-log-rate lower-bounds regret using sup_{nu in M^circ} kappa_nu on the STRICT INTERIOR subclass (not the closed-band frontier K); the closed-form K is the CLASS-LEVEL frontier object, and the export note must not conflate the strict-interior sup with the closed-band K. Align the two.

Discharge two final rigor gaps (field-tier result; math claim correct; do NOT weaken any statement). (1) radius_shrink_not_justified @ thm:global-log-rate: make lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk RADIUS-MONOTONE. State and prove it as: there exists eta_0>0 such that for EVERY eta in (0, eta_0] there is T_0(eta)<infinity with sup_{|h|<=eta} mathfrak_R_T(Alg, nu^(u,h)) >= c_0 S^2 dot_pi_nu(u)^2 J_nu(u)^{-1} log T for all T >= T_0(eta) and every Alg. (The Bayes/van-Trees argument already works for any fixed small eta with the prior supported on [-eta,eta]; just quantify over eta in (0,eta_0] rather than fixing one radius.) Then thm:global-log-rate may legitimately shrink eta to min(eta_0,eta_1) to stay inside the closed band M(underline_m,overline_m,underline_r) and STILL invoke the same Omega(log T) bound at the smaller radius — state this explicitly using the radius-monotone lemma. (2) Dependency ACYCLICITY (G4): no node may list ITSELF in its own depends_on. lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk must NOT reference lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk in its depends_on; keep the DAG acyclic (each lemma depends only on strictly earlier-derived facts). Ensure thm:instance-local-minimax -> lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk is the only new edge, with no back-edge.
